title: Table Management on Sybase
text: Table Management on Sybase The Sybase connector maps Uniface entities to Sybase tables. When Uniface entities are created on the fly or via the Create Table facility, a Sybase table, its indexes, and a group of stored procedures are also created. The connector can also be used to map Uniface entities to Sybase views. When you first attempt to access a table, Uniface needs to discover if it exists. If you have assigned an entity to a user.table name in an assignment file, Uniface queries sysobjects for all tables owned by the specified user. If you have not assigned an entity to a specific user in an assignment file, Uniface queries sysobjects for tables owned by either the current user or the database owner. If the table does not exist, Uniface creates the table and the indexes using the information from the application model. The table is created in the following way: If a transaction is running, a commit is issued. The table is created along with primary and candidate keys and using Sybase primary key and unique constraints. Non-unique indexes (which are not covered indexes) are created. Permission is granted to all users with the following Sybase statement: grant all on table to public Copy Stored procedures are created. If a previous version of the stored procedures exists, it is dropped before the new one is created. Execute permission on the procedure is granted to all users with the following Sybase statement: grant execute on procedure to public Copy Note: A table cannot be created on the fly if it is an overflow table. Sybase Temporary Tables Sybase temporary tables can be created in the Sybase temporary database, tempdb , only when procedures are disabled because Sybase does not allow procedures to be created on temporary tables. Temporary tables are dropped at the end of the transaction in which they were created, and can be used to store temporary data. The name of a temporary table must begin with a hash character (#). To create a temporary table, the following must be performed: The name of the Uniface entity must not start with a #. The entity must be renamed in the assignment table. The new name must start with a #, and the database name tempdb must be included. For example, tempdb..#temptable The SYB connector option disable procs must be entered in the assignment file. Overflow Tables Entities which contain one of the following Uniface data types require both a standard table and an overflow table: C* VC* U* VU* R* VR* Overflow tables cannot be created on the fly, so the Create Table utility must be used to create the them, and the Create Script utility must be run to create the referential integrity controls. Important: These utilities are not available in this release of Uniface 10. The name for an overflow table is the same name as the entity name with the prefix 'O'. If the name is longer than the Sybase SQL Server name limit of 30, the table name is truncated. The default length of each segment of an overflow table is 255 characters. You can increase this to 8000 characters using the widetable overflow connector option. There are no special considerations for inserts and updates on the associated normal table. If a record from the normal table is deleted, the overflow table record is also deleted. If Sybase is handling referential integrity (the dbms ri connector option is enabled), it performs the deletion. Otherwise, Uniface deletes for the overflow rows. Renaming Tables The assignment file can be used to rename the table name when the connector is required to access a table that is: In a database other than that of the current logon path. Owned by a user other than the current user. Has a name that differs from the lowercase version of the entity name, in the application model. The table names used in the assignment file are not converted to lower case unless they exactly match the entity name in the application model. Note: The current user must have the appropriate permissions on the table. Segmented Field Support The Sybase connectors support Uniface segmented fields by mapping them to Sybase text fields for segmented characters and image fields for segmented binary fields.
